ALL SPECIALTIES / DIETITIAN / GEORGIA

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Georgia Dietitian License

Georgia Dietitian license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Dietitian applications in Georgia run through Board of Examiners of Licensed Dietitians. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official College Transcripts - Must be sent directly from the degree-granting institution, showing degree and date awarded.
  • CDR Registration Card or Award Letter - Copy of your current, active Commission on Dietetic Registration credential.
  • ACEND Verification Statement - Verifying completion of supervised practice experience.
  • Secure & Verifiable Document and Affidavit of Citizenship - As required by Georgia law for professional licensing.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 15 to 20 Business Days
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Georgia offers licensure by endorsement for dietitians holding a valid, active, and unencumbered license in another state, provided that state's requirements are substantially equal to or greater than Georgia's. Applicants must submit their application, fee, verification of the out-of-state license sent directly from the issuing board, and proof of passing the CDR exam.

  1. STEP 1

    Create GOALS Account and Submit Online Application

    Register as a new user on the GOALS (Georgia Online Application Licensing System) portal. Complete the online Dietitian license application and pay the non-refundable application fee of $100.

    Fee: $85 · Timeline: 15 to 20 Business Days

  2. STEP 2

    Complete Criminal Background Check

    Upon submitting your initial application through GOALS, you will receive instructions from the Board to complete the mandatory criminal background check.

    Fee: $85 · Timeline: 15 to 20 Business Days

  3. STEP 3

    Submit Official Transcripts

    Arrange for official transcripts, verifying a baccalaureate or post-baccalaureate degree from an accredited institution with a major in human nutrition, dietetics, or food systems management, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Georgia Board of Examiners of Licensed Dietitians.

    Fee: $85 · Timeline: 15 to 20 Business Days

  4. STEP 4

    Verify Supervised Practice Experience

    Provide documentation confirming completion of a supervised practice experience of not less than 900 hours, approved by the Accreditation Council for Education in Nutrition and Dietetics (ACEND) of the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics, or an equivalent experience as determined by the Board.

    Fee: $85 · Timeline: 15 to 20 Business Days

  5. STEP 5

    Verify CDR Exam Passage

    Arrange for official verification of having passed the examination for dietitians administered by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) to be sent directly to the Georgia Board of Examiners of Licensed Dietitians.

    Fee: $85 · Timeline: 15 to 20 Business Days

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$75
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$85

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
Board of Examiners of Licensed Dietitians
Phone
(478) 207-2440
Website
sos.ga.gov/dietitians
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Dietitian renewal in Georgia follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years (by March 31, even years) + 30 CE hours
Renewal fee
$60
Continuing education
30 hours every 2 years

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a Georgia dietitian license cost?

The initial application fee is $85 (including a $10 processing fee). If you also request a provisional permit, add $25 for a total of $110. The biennial renewal fee is $60, due by March 31st of even-numbered years.

Which Georgia agency regulates dietitians?

The Georgia Board of Examiners of Licensed Dietitians, which operates under the Georgia Secretary of State, is the regulatory body for dietitian licensure in the state.

What are the continuing education requirements for Georgia dietitians?

You must complete 30 clock hours of continuing education during each two-year renewal cycle. As of 2026, all CE must be tracked and reported through CE Broker.

Does Georgia have reciprocity for dietitians?

Georgia has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Georgia?

Most endorsement applications take 15 to 20 Business Days.

Can I start working in Georgia while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$85

Processing time

15 to 20 Business Days

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · $25,

National exam

Registration Examination for Dietitians

Compact

Yes

Renewal

Every 2 years (by March 31, even years) + 30 CE hours · $60

Licensing board

Board of Examiners of Licensed Dietitians

sos.ga.gov/dietitians

(478) 207-2440
